# mcp-trino

> Composable MCP server for Trino, the federated SQL engine. Read-only by default with row caps and query timeouts. Optional semantic layer surfaces business descriptions, ownership, and sensitivity markers from DataHub or any metadata catalog. Imports as a Go library to compose custom MCP servers with auth, tenant isolation, and audit logging.

mcp-trino is a Model Context Protocol server that gives AI assistants safe, structured access to Trino and any Trino-compatible engine. Run SELECT and SHOW queries, browse catalogs, describe tables, and inspect execution plans. The standalone server works out of the box; the Go library lets you build custom MCP servers with middleware, interceptors, and transformers without forking. An optional semantic layer enriches schema results with business context.

## MCP Server

- [Server Overview](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/server/): How the standalone server works
- [Installation](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/server/installation/): Install and connect to AI clients (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor)
- [Configuration](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/server/configuration/): Environment variables, Trino credentials, SSL, timeouts
- [Tools Reference](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/server/tools/): Complete MCP tool descriptions and parameters
- [Multi-Server Setup](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/server/multi-server/): Connect to multiple Trino clusters from one install

## Semantic Layer

- [Semantic Overview](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/semantic/): Add business context to technical schemas
- [DataHub Provider](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/semantic/datahub/): Pull descriptions, owners, and sensitivity from DataHub
- [Static Provider](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/semantic/static/): YAML-defined semantic context
- [Custom Providers](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/semantic/custom/): Build your own metadata source
- [Caching](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/semantic/caching/): Reduce metadata round-trips

## Go Library

- [Library Overview](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/library/): Compose custom MCP servers from mcp-trino packages
- [Quick Start](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/library/quickstart/): Library usage in five minutes
- [Architecture](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/library/architecture/): Toolkit, client, and extensions layout
- [Extensibility](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/library/extensibility/): Middleware, interceptors, and transformers

## Reference

- [Tools API](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/reference/tools-api/): Detailed tool schemas and contracts
- [Configuration Reference](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/reference/configuration/): All environment variables and structured options
- [Security](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/reference/security/): Read-only mode, row limits, query timeouts, audit logging

## Key Capabilities

- Read-only by default. trino_query rejects non-read SQL; write operations require the opt-in trino_execute tool.
- Row caps and timeouts: default limit 1000 rows (max 10000), default timeout 120s (max 300s) per query.
- Multi-cluster: configure several Trino clusters via TRINO_ADDITIONAL_CONNECTIONS JSON.
- Semantic context: pull descriptions, ownership, and sensitivity from DataHub, static YAML, or custom providers.
- Composable Go library: middleware wraps tool execution; interceptors gate or rewrite requests; transformers redact or audit results.
- Stdio transport: ships as a standard MCP wire-protocol binary, no daemon, no HTTP server.
- SLSA Level 3 build provenance for supply-chain integrity.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install
brew install txn2/tap/mcp-trino

# Wire to Claude Code
claude mcp add trino \
  -e TRINO_HOST=trino.example.com \
  -e TRINO_USER=$USER \
  -e TRINO_PASSWORD=$PASS \
  -e TRINO_CATALOG=hive \
  -- mcp-trino
```

- [Troubleshooting](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/support/troubleshooting/): Common issues and fixes
- [Changelog](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/support/changelog/): Release history

- [Ecosystem](https://mcp-trino.txn2.com/ecosystem/): Sister MCP projects (mcp-s3, mcp-datahub, mcp-data-platform)
